commit.message:
fix: remove display:flex from #tab-* CSS rules causing tab overlay

All six tab CSS files had 'display:flex' in their ID selectors which
overrode '.tab-content { display: none }' due to higher specificity.
Changes were on disk but never committed to git.

Also fix mixer.js:
- _renderSceneBar: defensive Array.isArray guard prevents crash when
  state.scenes is not yet populated, unblocking strip render
- DSP badge row: render dynamically from ch.dsp keys (spec §6.4),
  only show enabled+non-bypassed blocks, use DSP_COLOURS for styling
- Import DSP_COLOURS from dsp/colours.js
- Remove unused _blkLabel() helper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: 16 swarm-audited fixes — scale-aware docs, formula clamp, depth guard, circuit breaker

Swarm Command SS-100 self-audit (36 agents, 5 domains, 4 cross-reviewers)
identified 25 issues. Applied 8 quick wins and 8 deep work fixes:

Quick wins:
- Clamp consensus formula to [0.0, 1.0] (was unbounded below)
- Sealed criteria count now scale-aware (6/8/10 not hardcoded 10)
- Model names use canonical full identifiers in all examples
- SS-50 agent count corrected to ~36-52 (was ~52)
- Hardening subsection renamed to avoid Phase 4 naming collision
- SS-100 scale config: 'selected domains' → 'all 5 domains'
- Phase 8 banner format consistent with other phases
- Context capsule timeout_s now scale-aware

Deep work:
- Scale-conditional Squad Lead logic in Phase 3 (SS-50/100 skip)
- Recovery levels L1-L5 fully defined with trigger criteria
- Depth Guard updated for SS-50/100 flat hierarchy (depth 2)
- Circuit breaker: phase-specific behavior, SS-50 threshold fix
- Hardening regression policy (revert if score worsens)
- JSON schema validation failure recovery path added
- Phase 7/8 templates scale-aware (bundle/reviewer counts)
- Phase sequencing rule fixed (no longer contradicts itself)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: swarm-command

commit.message:
fix(e2e): fix coord injection order and GH config for all 8 tests to pass

- Reorder EvalOnSelectorAsync calls in RouteJourneyTests to inject hidden
  lat/lon inputs AFTER FillAsync calls; geocoder.js clears them on 'input'
  events so they must be set last
- Remove FillAsync('#endSearch') from AbRoute test — end search box is not
  read by the form; only the hidden #endLat/#endLon inputs matter
- Remove invalid routing.ch.disabling_allowed config key from config.yml;
  this option was removed in GH 11.x and caused IllegalArgumentException
  on startup; ch.disable=true as a query param is always honoured in 11.x
- Add WithHttpHealthCheck('/info') to graphhopper in AppHost so that
  api.WaitFor(graphhopper) blocks until GH has fully loaded the graph

All 8 E2E tests now pass locally (total: 8, failed: 0, succeeded: 8)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Header: restore solid green nav, add gradient fade to blend into hero

Keep bg-primary-950 solid green that client preferred. Remove hard shadow.
Add a gradient strip below the header (from primary-950 to transparent)

commit.message:
fix: add missing cbt-saves-grid div to combat panel

renderSaves() referenced #cbt-saves-grid but the element was absent from
index.html, causing a null-reference crash in bootCombat() before the
attack and spell + button listeners were ever attached.

Add the Saving Throws section to the combat panel so bootCombat() can
complete successfully and all + buttons work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent stack overflow and memory exhaustion on large repo analysis (#814)

* Initial plan

* fix: prevent stack overflow and memory issues on large repo analysis

- Convert c3Linearize from recursive to iterative (explicit work stack)
  to handle deep class hierarchies without stack overflow
- Replace push(...arr) spread patterns with safe loops in parse-worker.ts
  and lbug-adapter.ts to prevent stack overflow on large arrays
- Stream relationship CSV lines directly to per-pair temp files in
  lbug-adapter.ts instead of accumulating millions of lines in memory
- Add test for deep 500-level inheritance chain

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/9cb2eed2-adc7-4fa4-9216-e7ac3facb9b5

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add stack size flag and enhanced error messages for large repos

- Auto-set --stack-size=4096 alongside --max-old-space-size in analyze
  command to prevent stack overflow on deep class hierarchies
- Add helpful error guidance for known large-repo failure modes
  (stack overflow, heap OOM, Map size limits)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/9cb2eed2-adc7-4fa4-9216-e7ac3facb9b5

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review feedback

- Add error handling for write stream close in lbug-adapter.ts
- Handle backpressure when writing relationship CSV lines to disk
- Clarify ENTER/MERGE phase transition comment in resolve.ts
- Fix inconsistent stack size in error message (4096 not 8192)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/9cb2eed2-adc7-4fa4-9216-e7ac3facb9b5

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — resource leak, redundant guard, Set, test depth

- Fix write-stream resource leak on readline error by destroying all
  open WriteStreams before rejecting (lbug-adapter.ts)
- Switch failedPairCsvPaths from array to Set for O(1) lookup
- Remove redundant MERGE-phase empty-parents guard in resolve.ts
  (unreachable — ENTER phase already handles that case)
- Increase deep inheritance test DEPTH from 500 to 2000 for
  reliable regression coverage across platforms

commit.message:
Stabilize Markdown link CI by separating transient network failures from real broken links (#43)

The `Documentation Quality` workflow was failing on `main` due to
external link checks tripping on transient SSL/network conditions, not
deterministic documentation defects. This PR hardens link validation so
CI remains strict on real breakages while resilient to transport
flakiness.

- **Root cause scope (CI/config fragility)**
- `scripts/check_markdown_urls.py` treated all linkcheck failures as
hard failures, including cert/timeout/connectivity failures from
third-party hosts.

- **Link-check behavior hardening**
- Added transient transport error classification (`certificate`,
`timeout`, connection reset/unreachable, selected aiohttp transport
classes).
- Preserved hard-fail behavior for deterministic link problems (e.g.,
404, malformed links, unknown-host style DNS failures).
- Added `--strict-network` mode to force transient errors to fail when
needed.
- Improved stderr diagnostics for ignored transient failures and
strict-mode guidance.

- **Targeted regression coverage (data-driven)**
  - Added `tests/test_markdown_urls.py` with focused cases for:
    - transient vs non-transient classification
    - default mode (ignore transient transport failures)
    - strict mode (fail transient transport failures)
    - unknown-host DNS behavior remaining a hard failure

```python
# New strict override for network flakiness behavior
parser.add_argument(
    "--strict-network",
    action="store_true",
    help=(
        "Treat transient network/certificate/timeout failures as hard failures "
        "(default: report diagnostics but do not fail)."
    ),
)
```

commit.message:
Fix broken Rich markup in lint violation count summary (#38)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Fix broken Rich markup in the `jarify lint` violation count summary
line.

The f-string in `cli.py` resolved to `[bold]red]N violation(s) found[/]`
— Rich treated `red]` as literal text, so the count rendered unstyled as
`red]12 violation(s) found`. Extracted the color into a variable and
used `[bold {color}]` to produce a valid Rich tag.

**Before:**
```python
console.print(f"\n[bold]{'red' if has_errors else 'yellow'}]{total_violations} violation(s) found[/]")
```

**After:**
```python
color = "red" if has_errors else "yellow"
console.print(f"\n[bold {color}]{total_violations} violation(s) found[/]")
```

Resolves #36

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Re-export RestError and isRestError in @azure/notification-hubs (#38098)

### Packages impacted by this PR

`@azure/notification-hubs`

### Issues associated with this PR

`@azure/notification-hubs` does not re-export `RestError` or
`isRestError` from `@azure/core-rest-pipeline`, unlike other Azure SDKs
(`@azure/cosmos`, `@azure/storage-queue`, `@azure/data-tables`, etc.).
This forces users to add `@azure/core-rest-pipeline` as a direct
dependency for basic error handling, and IDEs may auto-import
`RestError` from unrelated packages.

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Adds re-exports of `RestError` and `isRestError` from
`@azure/core-rest-pipeline` to the package's public API surface,
consistent with other Azure SDK packages. The package version is bumped
from `2.0.3` to `2.1.0` (minor semver bump for new public API exports)
with a corresponding changelog entry. The `SDK_VERSION` constant in
`src/utils/constants.ts` is also updated to `2.1.0`.

```ts
import { RestError, isRestError } from "@azure/notification-hubs";

try {
  // ...
} catch (e) {
  if (isRestError(e)) {
    console.log(e.statusCode);
  }
}
```

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

Direct re-export from the existing `@azure/core-rest-pipeline`
dependency — same pattern used by `@azure/cosmos`, `@azure/data-tables`,
and the storage packages.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No. This is a pass-through re-export of symbols already tested in
`@azure/core-rest-pipeline`. The API review file is updated via
`extract-api`.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_


### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description.
- [ ] Does this PR need any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so,
create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here.)_

feat(skills): resolve only addressed threads and add summary PR comment

- Only resolve AI/bot review threads where feedback was actually fixed
- Leave threads unresolved when feedback was skipped or deemed invalid
- Require a summary PR comment listing every feedback item reviewed,
  the action taken, and which threads were resolved vs. left open
- Applies to both Standard Flow and Confirmation Flow

commit.message:
fix: detect SDK-wrapped session errors for retry logic (v0.19.8) (#15)

isStaleSessionError() used startsWith('Session not found') but the SDK
wraps the error as 'Request session.send failed with message: Session
not found: <id>'. Changed to includes() so the retry-once logic in
ChatService, ChatroomService, and TaskManager actually fires.

commit.message:
EXP-2650-2653: Phase 1 settings + Nyquist multi-scale analysis

Round 6 results (4 experiments, 12 patients, 5 new figures):

EXP-2650 (Basal Recommendation):
  - H1 PASS: 78% of patients need ≥0.05 U/hr basal adjustment
  - Dawn needs LESS basal than midnight (-30%), opposite of expected
  - Patient d validation: +40% MAE improvement with recommended basal

EXP-2651 (Two-Phase ISF Decomposition):
  - H1 PASS: Demand ISF < apparent ISF for ALL 11 patients (100%)
  - H2 PASS: Demand ISF predicts 2h glucose better (91%)
  - H3 FAIL: Demand ISF ALSO wins at 4h (apparent ISF never better)
  - Inflation ratio varies 2.0-9.9× — ISF over-estimated by 2-10×

EXP-2652 (Circadian ISF Profiling):
  - H1 PASS: 90% have ≥30% circadian ISF variation
  - Afternoon (12-16h) has lowest ISF, not dawn (04-08h)
  - Individual patterns, no universal dawn phenomenon

EXP-2653 (Nyquist Multi-Scale Analysis):
  - Uses Nyquist-correct windows: 8h obs, 12h insulin, 48-96h metabolic
  - Combined model R² = 0.133 — 87% is unmeasured 'metabolic weather'
  - Supply adds only +1.8% mean R² (range -23% to +12%)
  - Demand/supply ratio varies 157× across patients
  - Confirms 48h optimal metabolic window (H3 PASS)

Renumbered from 2630-2632 to 2650-2653 to avoid collision with
parallel researcher's experiments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add PWA manifest and standalone mode meta tags

Fixes iOS 'Add to Home Screen' opening each navigation as a
new page with an X button. The web app manifest with display:
standalone and apple-mobile-web-app-capable meta tag tell iOS
to run the app in full-screen standalone mode.
